control_name: COSO principle: integrity and ethical values
notes: Organizational control; not a software concern
Organizational control
Audit events emitted for all operations via NATS; metrics endpoint for monitoring
Organizational control; KeyRack provides the technical controls to implement risk mitigations
PDP authorization on every operation; fail-closed design
Cedar policy engine enforces per-operation authorization. No implicit admin role. Every request evaluated.
Bearer token and mTLS authentication. Bootstrap token uses constant-time comparison (subtle crate)
PDP-based per-operation authorization. Key state machine prevents operations on disabled/destroyed keys
CLI tools for provisioning. Cedar policies manage access grants. No built-in user lifecycle management
Policy updates remove access. No automated integration with HR/IdP systems
Full key lifecycle: generate, enable, disable, rotate, schedule deletion, destroy. Versioned rotation. Configurable cryptoperiods.
gRPC/REST over TLS. PKCS#11 communication via local socket or TLS
AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption. HSM-backed key storage for production
Every operation emits audit events. Denied requests logged as AuthorizationDenied. Metrics endpoint available.
Metrics and audit events provide data for anomaly detection. No built-in anomaly detection rules
Incident response is an organizational process. KeyRack provides audit trail and key state for investigation
Key disable/destroy APIs enable rapid response. Cascade-disable for hierarchy-wide revocation. No automated incident playbooks
Organizational control; KeyRack has versioned releases and migration tooling
Crypto-agility via CryptoProvider trait and KeySpec enum. Rotation policies with configurable intervals
HA clustering available in commercial edition. FOSS is single-node
Metadata can be backed up. HSM key material backup depends on HSM vendor procedures
AES-GCM authentication tag ensures ciphertext integrity. Self-describing header prevents key/version confusion
Sensitive<T> wrapper for key material. Identity tags excluded from API responses. Encryption context hashed before storage
Key destruction with zeroization. Cascade-disable for hierarchy. Crypto-shredding capability
Organizational concern. KeyRack does not collect PII directly
AES-256-GCM encryption. Identity tag tokenization via BLAKE3 with per-tenant salt
Crypto-shredding via key destruction. Identity tags can be purged independently
Every operation (including denied requests) emits an audit event. Events include principal, action, resource, result, timestamp
NATS provides durable delivery. Signed audit receipts available in commercial edition (a partner). FOSS lacks cryptographic audit chain
Retention is a deployment/operational concern. NATS JetStream supports configurable retention
